rationale: X surfaces two grounded, actionable design notes (derive the component list from each pipeline's already-declared `_dit_modules` instead of a fourth hardcoded list, and flags that text-encoder LoRA is still a silent no-op) — the first echoes the ground truth's core theme (dsocek's push to drive component/key discovery from existing per-model metadata rather than ad-hoc lists), and X also explicitly credits that the requested before/after validation images were provided. Y's review is a thorough validation pass but yields zero findings or suggested changes, defaulting straight to APPROVE, so it misses the one substantive human concern entirely and offers nothing actionable for a reader to act on. Neither has a labeled LATENT GAP CHECK section.
